name: Zara Larsson
description:
'''Zara Maria Larsson''' ({{IPA|sv|ˈsɑ̂ːra ˈlɑ̌ːʂɔn|lang|sv-Zara Larsson.ogg}}; born 16 December 1997) is a Swedish singer and songwriter. She first rose to prominence in 2008 after winning the [[Talang 2008|second season]] of the Swedish talent show competition ''{{lang|sv|[[Talang (Swedish TV show)|Talang]]}}''. Larsson signed with [[TEN Music Group]] in 2012 and released her debut extended play (EP), ''Introducing'', the following year. Its single "[[Uncover (song)|Uncover]]" charted at number one in Sweden, where it was certified [[Music recording certification|6× Platinum]], and in Norway. Her debut studio album, ''[[1 (Zara Larsson album)|1]]'', spawned the Swedish top-ten singles "[[Carry You Home (Zara Larsson song)|Carry You Home]]" and "[[Rooftop (song)|Rooftop]]".

Larsson later signed a three-year contract with [[Epic Records]] in the United States, through which her debut international album, ''[[So Good (Zara Larsson album)|So Good]]'' (2017), was released. It became her second number-one album in Sweden, while charting in the top-ten in seven other countries. Its lead single "[[Lush Life (Zara Larsson song)|Lush Life]]" charted in the top-five in 18 countries and became one of the best-selling songs of the 2010s in the United Kingdom. The album featured the follow-up singles "[[Never Forget You (Zara Larsson and MNEK song)|Never Forget You]]" (with [[MNEK]]), which reached number one in Sweden, and "[[I Would Like]]", which reached number two in the United Kingdom. She was featured on [[Clean Bandit]]'s 2017 single "[[Symphony (Clean Bandit song)|Symphony]]", which became a number-one song in the UK and topped the Swedish and Norwegian charts. Her third studio album, ''[[Poster Girl (album)|Poster Girl]]'' (2021), was led by the single "[[Ruin My Life]]", which was certified gold in the US and the UK.

In 2022, Larsson left TEN Music Group and acquired her music catalogue. Her fourth studio album, ''[[Venus (Zara Larsson album)|Venus]]'', was released in 2024 under Epic Records and her own label, Sommer House. Larsson's fifth studio album, ''[[Midnight Sun (Zara Larsson album)|Midnight Sun]]'', was released in 2025 to widespread critical acclaim. Its [[Midnight Sun (Zara Larsson song)|title track]] became her fifth entry on the US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]], peaking at number 69, and earned Larsson a [[Grammy Award]] nomination for [[Grammy Award for Best Dance Pop Recording|Best Dance Pop Recording]].
